I never locked my TX, now that I have received rewards the wallet has more than 15,000 ZNN... can I still lock the TX? I have disabled staking in my wallet so nothing should be spent of what I received either way.

reply_to.content:
How to make a 15000 transaction:
1. Open Wallet.
2. Go to the Receive tab.
3. Type Pillar-1 in the Label field.
4. Click Request.
5. From the new window copy your new Address.
6. Go to the Send tab, paste your address in the Pay To field.
7. Type 15000 in the Amount field.
8. Click Send.

At this point you should have secured your Pillar, but you need to set it up in order to receive rewards.
If you're setting up the Pillar right now, you can stop. Find the guide right here: https://github.com/zenonnetwork/ZNN-Node-deployer
Otherwise, continue with the next steps.
___________
Now we need to lock that TX so it doesn't get spent by Staking.
First step is making sure we have Coin Control enabled.
1. Go to Settings.
2. Click Options.
3. Go to Wallet tab.
4. Make sure the Enable coin control features checkbox is checked.
5. Press OK.
___________
And now we're locking the the TX. 
Wait 5 minutes after following the instructions mentioned earlier and then follow these steps.
1. Go to Send tab again.
2. Click Open Coin Control...
3. Select List Mode.
4. Find the line that says Pillar-1 in the Received with label column
5. Right click that line.
6. Click Lock unspent.

reply_to.content: Responses to this concern: 1) the rewards are only certain to go until the NoM and therefore act as proportional placeholders to reward early buying and hodling but aren’t necessarily a part of the future business model/tokenomics (aka no promise of perpetual “free money” like many masternode projects), relatedly 2) the pre-NoM available supply is so low that there are only so many “free ZNN” to be available from rewards and therefore again its less of an unsustainable dilutive promise and rather a race to accumulate as much proportional ownership of the limited pre-NoM supply, and also relatedly 3) because of these tokenomics and eventual transition away from this placeholder chain, the desire to sell rewards which is typically very high with masternodes does/will not exist here, at least nowhere close to the typical degree seen in masternode projects. It’s almost completely unfair to compare Zenon to a “typical masternode project” even absent our hopes/positive suspicions

messages.content: https://twitter.com/ZenonOrg/status/1543768545619738626?s=20

Thanks for the feedback, I designed the .org 🙂 I think the only thing that’ll be official about the zenon.org brand will be that many of its sub-domain tools will be operated by the contributing community (ie docs site, newsletter issues etc). IMO it’s important for an entity to take leadership to aggregate the whole as one — and I do plan to try to outshine the zenon.network website with my work on the zenon.org (and related tools). 

We had a choice to centralize the entire zenon.org brand, and chose against it i.e. many of the stuff we ask for collabs in the forums, could’ve all be done centrally by us — we have the funds to hire if we wanted-to. Instead we preferred to let community develop their own A-Z proposals using the tools and brand name (subdomains) to make the ecosystem more unified.

We may also lead to collabs with other pillar operators. I’m inspired by the fact that some pillar operators are willing to contribute to a pillar-owned brand name, for the well-being of network growth. I will find a balance of featuring contributing entities on the upcoming org, including pillars that do compete with ZenonORG pillars. It’s tricky, but I’m sure it can be done.

messages.content: The .org helped a lot to direct traffic, but really the trick was to be loud in the outbound. I don’t know if you remember TC in 2019-2020 — they were crazy loud, and gained a huge community. And hence why I’m pushing for these spaces of organized content, whether it be via a well built docs site, or curated newsletter issues from community experts on various topics. The .org brand would do its thing to be loud alongside community and distribute the content in relevant outbound feeds. With an attribute.me integration, whoever shares any zenon.org landing page will also be attributed any conversions that may have occured on zenon.org — or Zenon ecosystem sites i.e. imagine someone builds a DEX and integrates in the ecosystem attribute systems, if someone shared a link to any zenon.org page, and incoming traffic ends up performing swaps on the DEX, that user who originally shared that link, would be attributed the swap conversions. All in a public dashboard on Attribute.me. Just so many opportunities with working together as one ecosystem rather than duplicate tools trying to solve the same problem. ZenonOrg will be nothing more than a tool to get traffic from point A to point Z. Looking forward to being loud once we roll out the .org site!

reply_to.content: I think zenon.org is the perfect place to grow the community.  Who knows how Pillar "competition" will unfold in the future.  At this point we need to expand our footprint and .org is a great place to do it.  I have no ideas how the TC.org numbers look, but it must have been the largest driver of early adoption (total guess).

On intel macOs it also works fine for me.
https://twitter.com/mehowbrainz/status/1647051636164968449?s=20

A few things missing in this strategy:

1) Proof of concept to validate whether the content/ad converts engagment into the next stage of the funnel (the landing page).

2) Once the ad ends where is the user driven to? A landing page? which one? What’s the action?

3) What other metrics will be measured past ad views to deem that the campaign lead to new value to the network? Page views of the landing page? Clicks on whichever Attribute event? Given the Attribute funnels for syrius aren’t completed yet, we actually can’t know whether someone delegated, staked, installed the wallet just yet.

4) To say that it will bring 1-5K new users is a massive assumption, nor will we ever be able to prove that they’re participants (given the lack of integrations atm). Maybe landing page viewers, maybe? But users, not sure about that at all. I’d think a fraction of 1-5K if we’re lucky.

5) What’s the participant you’re focusing on in the campaign? New wallet installs? New pillars? New devs? We can’t target them all in 1 campaign. Such ideas are too general and IMO we’re past that stage of assumptions, we need proposals which at the bare minimum can answer every point the guidelines suggest: https://forum.zenon.org/t/performance-marketing-proposal-guidelines/918?u=mehowbrainz

6) Is YouTube friendly to blockchain/crypto ads? Might want to re-evaluate that assumption. Every major network including Twitter are difficult on crypto ads….

7) Ad spend requires retargeting to drive back value from the ad spent, which hasn’t been integrated yet into zenon.org funnels nor Attribute. Without it, once you spend and that user clicks, you have no way to drive him back to your content/channels.

I’m all for the excitement to drive awareness to the network, but IMO we’re not ready yet for ad spend. You can easily burn funds and have no ability to measure success if other tooling isn’t implemented/ready yet — and so far the zenon.org funnels don’t have the syrius / walletconnect integrations (nor are they developed yet), so for that reason I would argue to hold off from even considering ad spend at this stage. Anyone who does will get grilled on the lack of ability to justify success.

A better strategy would be for the community to roll up their sleeves, go on Tweet Deck and other social networks/tools — and begin to listen-for conversations that are fitting for Zenon, and paste their Attribute links in mix with contextual content that adds value to generate that traffic and engagement. You’d be surprised how many impressions tweets can gets if they’re positioned in the right conversations / say the right things. 

Attribute campaigning tutorial: https://twitter.com/AttributeLink/status/1644361381074837505

Many Attribute campaigners are driving some traffie, we can crank this up by 100x easily just with focus and dedicated by the community to become strategic marketers.

Ads are good for top of funnel awareness. But we just don’t have all the tools in place to measure full cycle yet…

Greetings, everyone!

I wanted to take a moment to share an important decision I have made. 

After careful consideration, I have come to the conclusion that it's time for me to step down from my role as a community manager.

I firmly believe that this is the most appropriate course of action for the current stage of the project.

There seems to be some confusion among certain members who mistakenly perceive me as a "founding team" member, and there's a risk of my words being misconstrued as official statements, which has never been my intention in any way.

In light of this, I have chosen @ZeroX3639 as my successor. He possesses exceptional technical knowledge, demonstrates strong motivation, has displayed leadership qualities on numerous occasions, and enjoys immense respect within our community. Above all, he's fully committed to ensuring the success of the project.

Working alongside all of you has been an incredibly enriching experience. I'm sincerely grateful for the opportunities I have had. 

Please rest assured that although I'm stepping down from my current role, I'm not leaving and will continue to be an active and engaged member of this outstanding community, albeit in a different capacity where I can contribute in new and valuable ways.

Additionally, I would like to inform you that I still have possession of the remaining WarpDrive funds, amounting to 58,717.70 QSR. I'm in the process of transferring this amount to AZ, and I'll promptly share the transaction ID with you zoon.

reply_to.content:
If you are asking for critique and opinion I can provide. 
As a disclaimer I am not trying to enforce my opinion as the correct one or correct in general.

First, I think there is an overemphasis on liquidity. 
Yes, it is important for buying and selling in larger quantities - but more liquidity doesn't necessarily draw new users. 
To the contrary many alt-coin investors and traders prefer finding low/medium liquidity situation and riding the pa of volatility. 
After all, meme coins aren't sought after because of their liquidity.

As per everything the fulcrum should be centered around bitcoin and how we can anchor into bitcoin. Why build a liquidity base to compete with others if can leverage the gigantic base bitcoin provides?

Secondly, the sales angle is far too on the nose for my liking - it reeks of corporate machinations which is not popular in crypto (or anywhere outside of corporate offices), that being said it may work or not - but fundamentally it's far more difficult to generate a new user base than taping into an existing one. 

If we had a few good articles that explained and illustrated how bitcoin scaling and interoperability would work in detail and our roadmap to achieve it and these were shared throughout bitcoin crypto twitter and reddit this would be be far more impactful than sales funnels and affiliate programs in my opinion.
 

And as an aside - for anybody trying to shill with affiliate links. A nice tactic is to show the user how to generate their own affiliate link so they get 3% bonus to their purchase. This will make them far more likely to purchase and will reflect more positively on your yourself and your shilling efforts. 

Anyway that's just my opinion, the fulcrum should be bitcoin and anything else is just a waste of time.

PREREQUISITES

messages.content:
Community Notice: Important Security Incident and Apologies

I am writing to you today to inform you of a recent security incident involving my Discord account. A user, posing as a journalist named Abdul, contacted me on Discord, expressing interest in press inquiries related to our community (posing as ournetwork.xyz -- a legitimate publication it seems). He directed me to join the OurNetwork Discord server to make a ticket for our discussion. The served seemed legitimate, posing as a workspace for the brand and private space for its subscribers. Upon joining, I was prompted to verify my account using an external link. The verification process appeared authentic, and without suspecting foul play, I proceeded. But the verification was in fact a "self-XSS (Cross Site Scripting)" attack. It's designed to deceive users into executing harmful JavaScript code in their web browser, particularly in the context of a web application like Discord.

In my naivety, I assumed this external verification was a special requirement for that particular server. I've since learned that Discord does not use external sites for server verification, and realizing this was a sophisticated hacking attempt, I feel responsible for not being more vigilant.

Thankfully, my password wasn't changed because I had 2FA enabled. My email address and phone number (which wasn't associated with my account) remained unaffected. However, the breach could have had far more severe consequences. I want to emphasize that all my accounts are highly secured. This incident was not a result of inadequate security setup but rather a sophisticated type of attack that exploited a different kind of vulnerability - my trust in the legitimacy of external verification processes.

I sincerely apologize for any concerns this might have caused. This incident was a stark reminder of the sophistication of online threats and the need for constant vigilance.

Please be aware that Discord does not use external sites for server verification and always be skeptical of such requests, especially external verifications. Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for an added layer of security.

I take full responsibility for this oversight and promise to be more cautious in the future. Let's all use this incident as a learning opportunity to strengthen our community's security and awareness.

Stay safe and secure,
mehowz

messages.content: We call those preframe bridges in marketing: articles, news, blog posts, videos, emails, presell pages... designed in a funnel form to keep the user engaged. Next they get qualified via some squeeze page (can be an incentive that they'll get the 1% bonus on bridging). The qualification is asking them for some basic info, their email or TG handle so we have something to work with (retargetting) -- and also have retargeting ad networks to show them more Zenon in the outbound as they browse the web (based on what the systems knows they read). The whole idea of funnels is to shortcut the process to convert someone into becoming a participant into the network, without sounding salesy or scammy. Make it natural. Content creators are good for the preframe bridge creation, but they have to know what to lead their traffic into. I will demonstrate examples of this shortly as I roll out first funnels for core needs i.e. bridging, buying wznn etc.

THORChain held a lot of hands with community enabled spaces like thornoobs and lp university -- but I think we can do better and make it so simple where it's automated to an extent. Community can kick in for support where needed. With metrics we'll figure out where the weak stages of the funnels are.

For those on desktop atm, here's an interactive prototype for the "Download the Full Deck" funnel:
https://zenonorg815913.invisionapp.com/console/share/ZE3CSHN8F9A/987756331

I use a lead magnet for the first step to capture their email address. Ideally I'd also like their Telegram handle as well, but  I don't want to overwhelm them so I prioritize what's most important for me: getting their email so that I could automatically nurture them over the long run. I will be testing methods in some future to nurture automatically via Telegram as well.

Therefore I keep the "give me your telegram handle" on the second step once the user has entered their email address. I use an OTO page (one-time-offer). It's designed to upsell and get them to give their TG handle in exchange for the 1% bonus activation. That bonus would get activated regardless because all ZenonOrg landing pages automatically enable it for users as soon as they land, but they don't know that. So that's an opportunity for me to get something extra. I do give them the option to skip at this upsell step however.

At the third step, I initiative the download of the Full Deck. I then try to upsell them again, this time bridging them into the "Buy wZNN on Uniswap" step. Their bonus is activated already, so they may feel inclined to go through with the purchase after they've read the deck? We will see.

The 4th step would be to lead them into the bridge once we qualify that they've purchased wZNN at the 3rd step.

At every step I know what users did, or didn't do. Since I have their email address I can begin to nurture email campaigns to help them resume their onboarding into the network.

These funnels will be brandable to ecosystem brands i.e. zenon.info, build_ etc. They will also be added to the Attribute library so anyone can promote any flavour.

I'm developing a dozen of these, for all types of downloads i.e. syrius, nodes, guides, buying wZNN and more.

After chatting w/ a few people off this thread thought I would summarize my thoughts on the recent AZ discussions as concisely as I can - 
1. Anything worth submitting to AZ should have provable value. If there is no value, why are we doing it? 
2. There is more than one way to measure value. If you are submitting for marketing (since that is the hot topic) then either a) use the infra that mehowz built to prove value or b) propose another way. c) saying it doesn’t have value or we cant track it is either a lazy answer or proof we shouldn’t be paying for it. Read lean start-up to get some ideas here or just use AI. 
3. Letting your emotions drive financial decisions is almost always a bad idea. If you submit for $20k and people say no you can either get pissed and walk away with $0 OR you can engage in dialogue to address concerns and walk away with something. Foregoing thousands of dollars to say “fuck you” is a bad decision, especially considering the mark it leaves when / if you try to submit future AZ proposals. 
4. I am just one vote so while I may be vocal here all of this is just my opinion and overindexing on someone that holds one vote is probably a bad decision too...

We don’t just want Open Source. We want Free and Open Source. FOSS.

The spirit of AZ is that if something is paid for then others should be able to build off of it.

However that doesn’t there can’t be restrictions/requirements.

Syrius is licensed under a permissive MIT license. You can do almost anything you want with it. 

go-zenon is under a copyleft GPL-3. NAL but essentially what this means is that derivative works must also be released under a copyleft license.

For content, there are probably also similar licenses where derivative works are fine, but they must also be open and allowed to be built off of.

Dear Fellow Aliens

It has been an honor and privilege to help manage this Telegram channel since Sigli departed.  We have an amazing group of administrators and community members who really care about this project. I'm honored to work with you.  After careful consideration and discussion with other community members, my time as "owner" of the channel will come to an end.

Now that zenon.network is no longer the identity of this project we have the opportunity to archive this channel and build new communication channels started and managed by other community members not associated with the project founding.

While back links to this channel are important, building new community channels not associated with the founding devs is more important.  My strong preference is to archive this channel and make one final post saying goodby.

However, I understand some community members are opposed to this and their voice should be heard.

The community has 30 days (until 27 May 2024 at 12 PM UTC) to propose a new owner of this channel and get them approved by AZ vote.  If a new owner is approved I will transfer ownership to them and focus my efforts on other channels and development opportunities within NoM.  If a new owner cannot be approved within 30 days I will archive the channel and make one final post.  If the community wants to make that final post instead they should submit an AZ and get the post approved before the expiration of 30 days.

In full disclosure, I plan to vote NO on any new owner.  While there are many community members who could do a great job managing this channel I strongly believe we need to move beyond the legacy communication channels Kaine left us and start fresh. 

If anyone attacks me or threatens me because of this decision I reserve the right to accelerate the archival of this channel.
